channel.title: From Rocks to Rockets
channel.description: From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group is an AI-powered podcast exploring the industries building the future — from power, critical minerals, and data centers to AI infrastructure, bitcoin mining, nuclear, robotics, and aerospace. We cover the hidden physical systems behind the next industrial era and explain how raw materials become the technologies shaping tomorrow. For builders, investors, and operators who want to understand where the world is really headed. The future won't be built in code alone.
channel.itunes:author: Alera Group

item.title: Your Old Servers Are a Gold Mine — Literally
item.description:
Korea Zinc is in talks with US tech companies to extract rare earth elements from retired data center hardware — and building a $7.4 billion smelter in Tennessee to process it.
From Rocks to Rockets by Alera Group — the podcast about the industries building the future. New episodes daily covering AI infrastructure, data centers, critical minerals, nuclear/SMRs, energy, robotics, aerospace, and the geopolitics connecting all of it.
Chapters: 0:00 Cold Open 0:07 Intro 0:28 The Big Reveal — Korea Zinc 1:01 Rare Earths Inside Your Servers 1:45 Korea Zinc's $7.4B Smelter 2:25 Pentagon Critical Mineral Scramble 3:09 US-China Rare Earth Geopolitics 4:18 The Circular Supply Chain 5:25 The Country That Wins 6:09 Outro
This episode was produced with AI-assisted tools including AI-generated voice delivery and visuals. All content, research, and editorial direction by Jason Sher and Shane Smith.

item.title: The Pentagon's Deadline Just Hit. America Still Can't Mine Its Own AI Chips.
item.description: Today the Pentagon's deadline for critical minerals supply proposals expired. The same week, NVIDIA announced $1 trillion in AI chip orders through 2027. Every chip requires minerals America doesn't produce. The gap has never been wider. Hosts Jason Sher and Shane Smith break down the collision between AI demand, mineral supply, and geopolitics. This podcast is AI-generated and produced by Flova AI for Alera Group.

item.title: America's Chip Comeback: Intel, AMD, and the Race to Build Semiconductors on U.S. Soil
item.description:
The most advanced semiconductor ever manufactured on American soil just shipped — from Intel's Fab 52 in Chandler, Arizona. Meanwhile, AMD and Flex are assembling the most powerful AI GPU platforms in Austin, Texas. In this episode, Jason Sher and Shane Smith break down the U.S. semiconductor renaissance — Intel's 18A breakthrough, the $19 billion in CHIPS Act funding backing it, AMD's new GPU assembly line, and why geographic diversity away from Taiwan is now a competitive advantage for the entire data center industry.
Chapters: 0:00 Cold Open 0:08 Intro 0:30 Intel's 18A Breakthrough 1:20 Panther Lake and Fab 52 2:15 AMD and Flex in Austin 3:10 CHIPS Act and the Money Behind It 4:05 The Taiwan Risk 4:55 What This Means for Data Centers 5:40 Outro
From Rocks to Rockets is AI-produced using our cloned voices. The research, the analysis, and the opinions are ours. Alera Group — Emerging Industries.

item.title: Musk's Terafab: The $25 Billion Bet to Build America's Chip Independence
item.description:
Last night Elon Musk formally announced the TERAFAB project -- a joint Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI venture to build the largest private semiconductor facility in American history. Located at the North Campus of Giga Texas in Austin, the Terafab targets over one terawatt of compute per year at 2-nanometer process technology. 80% of output is destined for space, 20% for ground. Jason Sher breaks down the announcement, the $25-45 billion price tag, the AI5 chip, ASML constraints, and what this means for US industrial independence. A solo episode.
Chapters: 0:00 Cold Open 0:10 Intro 0:30 The Announcement 1:30 Why Tesla Needs Its Own Fab 2:45 The Space Angle 3:45 Can They Actually Pull It Off? 5:00 What This Means for Reshoring 6:00 Outro
From Rocks to Rockets is AI-produced using our cloned voices. The research, the analysis, and the opinions are ours. Alera Group -- Emerging Industries.
